candidates.title: Premium Heritage Apparel Identity System & Factory-Ready Vector Engineering
candidates.tools: Illustrator — for print-ready CMYK output and scalable vector artwork
candidates.brief: 1. Project Overview & ObjectiveBrand Status: Pre-launch Premium Apparel Startup (Name hidden for Intellectual Property protection). Niche: High-end, heritage-inspired athletic apparel (aesthetic alignment: upscale country club, vintage collegiate, luxury athleisure). Objective: Translate existing conceptual 3D/AI design mockups into a finalized, technically precise, and production-ready 2D vector asset system. Manufacturing Intent: The final vector graphics must be meticulously engineered to accommodate physical apparel manufacturing techniques, specifically high-density direct embroidery, woven patches with merrowed borders, and debossed hardware. Note on Assets: Conceptual designs will be shared strictly with shortlisted candidates during the interview phase under a mutual NDA. 2. candidates.title: Modern Fashion Brand Logo Design
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork
candidates.brief: I’m launching a new fashion label and need a sleek, modern logo that instantly tells customers we’re current, stylish, and high-quality. The aesthetic should feel fresh rather than vintage or overly minimalist—think confident typography, balanced negative space, and an emblem or wordmark that will look just as good stitched on fabric as it does online. I’m completely open on colours. Feel free to explore palettes that pair well with luxury streetwear or contemporary ready-to-wear collections; I’m happy to be surprised if the tones reinforce the brand mood. Here’s what I’d like from you: • Three distinct modern concepts to start, each explained briefly so I understand the thinking behind the shapes, type, and colour choices. • Two rounds of refinements on the concept I choose. • Final hand-off in vector (AI, EPS) plus high-resolution PNG and JPEG, all with transparent and solid-background

candidates.title: Realistic Textile Embroidery Design
candidates.brief: I'm looking for a skilled embroidery designer to create a realistic logo. Requirements: - Realism style - Medium size (3-6 inches) - Experience with logo designs and embroidery techniques Ideal skills and experience: - Proficiency in embroidery digitization - Strong understanding of realistic design elements - Attention to detail and quality craftsmanship - Ability to provide samples or a portfolio Please provide your portfolio with relevant work.

candidates.title: Authentic Italian Restaurant Logo Design (Cavatelli)
candidates.tools: Illustrator · Photoshop — Scalable vector logo that works in horizontal and stacked lock-ups across signage, menus, packaging and digital (Illustrator); Photoshop for mockups.
candidates.brief: Logo design for Cavatelli, an authentic Italian kitchen and wine bar. The design should feel rooted in Italian tradition while remaining fresh and versatile, evoking the charm of a beloved Italian eatery. Requirements: authentic, welcoming design reflecting Italian heritage and craftsmanship; strong typography with character and personality (serif, vintage-inspired or custom lettering encouraged); an icon, crest, monogram or emblem is optional; warm, rich, inviting colour palette; functional across menus, signage, merchandise, packaging and digital; effective in both horizontal and stacked formats. The logo should complement the Carbonara and Cappellini brands while establishing a unique personality. Designer should have restaurant/hospitality branding experience and a strong typography/logo portfolio.

candidates.title: Luxury, One-of-a-Kind Logo / STRYNO
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork
candidates.brief: I need a logo that immediately signals exclusivity—something no one has ever seen before. The brand will debut in the construction arena yet must be versatile enough to cross into any industry I decide to touch, so the mark has to feel universal and aspirational at the same time. The logo should be primarily be based and created on and around the company name: STRYNO (exact emblem shape is attached and eagle with the emblem attached could be used as well. Coloring from emblem could be disregarded as attachment serves as sample only ) Visual direction Think strong heritage fused with modern minimalism: the elegance of an old-world emblem, refined to a timeless, contemporary silhouette. A signature-style wordmark or a bold initial-based crest could work; I’m open to whichever route best captures that “only one in the world” aura. Rich finishes, subtle luxury cues, and perfect geometry are
